rationale.R1.2: A general reaction to the section centrally includes checking whether the methodological setup, reported measurements, and conclusions form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes convergence, work, and robustness claims that should be assessed against the reported table and experimental descriptions.
rationale.R1.5: The delegation invites feedback on the section as a section, including repetition, ordering, and narrative progression across paragraphs.
rationale.R1.6: The artifact contains repeated phrasing and sentence-level presentation issues, making prose quality a central expected responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of broad feedback must disclose limits imposed by the absence of Table 1 and surrounding paper context.
rationale.RX.2: The section draws preference and robustness conclusions, so avoiding unsupported strengthening of those claims is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within evaluation of the paper section rather than executing edits or expanding the study.
rationale.RX.4: Feedback should avoid encouraging misleading scientific claims or unsupported methodological recommendations.
rationale.RX.5: Any assessment should remain traceable to the provided artifact and distinguish visible text from missing evidence.
